deliverables: Parti diagram and design narrative (1–2 pages)
Preliminary floor plan sketches (1:200 scale minimum)
Site placement / orientation concept — horizon-axis orientation documented
3D conceptual massing model
Material and color direction board (preliminary) — salt-air and wind-resistant material palette
Space arrangement diagram showing all 10 programmed spaces (320 sqm total)
scope_phases.notes: Coastal Pacific site conditions inform all conceptual decisions. Horizon-as-living-condition design intent drives site placement and orientation. Wind load study required before construction phase.

deliverables: Floor plans all levels (1:100 scale)
Elevations all four facades (1:100)
Building sections minimum 2 (1:100)
Roof plan
Site plan with property boundaries, setbacks, access, parking (3,000 sqm site, Cerritos, BCS)
Interior design criteria document — includes coordination notes for TBD interior design collaborator
Window and door schedule
Preliminary structural coordination sketch — coastal wind load requirements noted
scope_phases.notes: Structural engineer joins this phase. Coastal wind load study results (when received) inform structural preliminary coordination. Pool deck placement determined based on solar orientation and wind study.

deliverables: Structural engineering drawings integrated with architectural set — wind load requirements for coastal BCS site incorporated
Electrical single-line diagram and panel schedules
Lighting layout plan with fixture specifications
Plumbing and hydraulic layout
Irrigation plan included — coastal native scrub-to-sand transition zone (27 sqm landscape zone)
Solar systems layout — solar is in scope per special features; client decision on final configuration required by end of Phase 2
Foundation plan and details — coastal conditions may require enhanced foundation spec pending wind load study
Landscape architecture coordination: in scope via TBD collaborator; Oficio Taller coordinates referral and integration
scope_phases.notes: Wind load study results must be received before structural drawings are finalized. Coastal zone permit application is a parallel prerequisite for Phase 6.

deliverables: Fully coordinated construction drawing set (architectural + structural + MEP)
Detail sheets: wall sections, stairs, roof details, openings — salt-air resistant materials specified
Finish schedule (all surfaces, all rooms) — coastal-grade material specifications
Door and window schedule with specifications — wind load rated per coastal BCS requirements
Built-in furniture drawings (kitchen, closets, built-in shelving)
Technical specifications document
Bid package preparation (executive plans + spec document sent to minimum 2 contractors)
Bid comparison matrix (contractor name, total, key line items, timeline, notes)
Recommendation with rationale
Local contractor cost validation against current BCS coastal residential benchmarks
scope_phases.notes: Minimum 2 bids required. Local contractor experience with coastal construction and salt-air material handling is an evaluation criterion.

deliverables: Building permit application package (architectural plans + structural calculations + forms)
Coastal zone permit application — Municipio de Los Cabos / SEMARNAT zone permit for Pacific-facing site in Cerritos, BCS
Submission to municipal authority (Municipio de Los Cabos)
Tracking and correction management until building permit issued
Wind load study submission to structural authority if required
scope_phases.notes: Coastal zone permit (Zona Federal Marítimo Terrestre / SEMARNAT) adds lead time. Timeline is authority-dependent. Permit corrections may require additional submissions. This is a critical path dependency for construction start.

payment_schedule.name: Concept Design Approved
payment_schedule.trigger_event: Client written approval of Phase 1 conceptual design

payment_schedule.name: Executive Plans Approved
payment_schedule.trigger_event: Executive plans package approved by client

responsibilities_matrix.responsible_party: Solar contractor / MEP engineer (Oficio Taller coordinates)
TBD landscape collaborator (Oficio Taller refers and coordinates)
TBD interior design collaborator (Oficio Taller refers and coordinates)
Client (required document — not included in Oficio Taller scope)
Client (required document — not included in Oficio Taller scope)
responsibilities_matrix.reviewed_by: Oficio Taller reviews on receipt; informs structural engineering

exclusions: Structural, electrical, plumbing, and civil engineering fees — client contracts engineers directly; Oficio Taller coordinates only
Permit fees, municipal taxes, SEMARNAT coastal zone fees, or government charges of any kind
Contractor selection or construction contracts — Oficio Taller recommends via bid comparison; client decides
Furniture, art, décor, or movable equipment not shown in built-in furniture drawings
Topographic survey — required before construction; client responsibility to provide
Wind load study — required for structural engineering; client responsibility to procure
Legal review of property title, Zona Federal Marítimo Terrestre boundaries, or easements
Construction cost guarantees — budget estimates are preliminary; final cost determined by contractor bids
Independent landscape architecture services — Oficio Taller will coordinate with TBD collaborator but landscape design fees are separate
Independent interior design services — Oficio Taller will coordinate with TBD collaborator but interior design fees are separate
Pool construction management — pool structure design is included in architectural scope; pool contractor and equipment are client responsibility
Hydrologic or soil study, if required by municipal authority — client responsibility

revision_assumptions.phase_1: 2 rounds of client revision included. Each additional round billed at MXN 8,000/day.
revision_assumptions.phase_2: 2 rounds of client revision included. Each additional round billed at MXN 10,000/day.
revision_assumptions.phase_3: 1 round of structural coordination revision included. Changes driven by client program additions are billed additionally.
revision_assumptions.phase_4: 1 round of coordination revision included. Client changes after executive plan approval require a change order.
revision_assumptions.phase_5: Bid comparison matrix is a single deliverable; contractor selection is client decision.
revision_assumptions.phase_6: Permit corrections required by authority are managed within scope. Redesign work triggered by permit corrections that require architectural changes will be scoped as a change order.

project_type_clauses.title: Standard Residential Scope
project_type_clauses.text: This project is a private standalone residential building on an individually owned lot. All design decisions are made exclusively between the client and Oficio Taller unless otherwise noted. No HOA, covenant, or third-party design review applies unless disclosed by the client before contract signing.

project_type_clauses.title: Client-Initiated Program Changes
project_type_clauses.text: Changes to the approved program requested by the client after a phase milestone has been approved will be scoped and billed as a change order. Oficio Taller will not absorb rework costs from client-initiated changes.

project_type_clauses.title: Site Condition Discovery
project_type_clauses.text: Design is based on information provided by the client and publicly available municipal records. If site conditions discovered during construction (soil, utilities, encroachments) require design changes, these will be scoped as a change order.

project_type_clauses.title: Electronic Signature
project_type_clauses.text: This document is valid when signed electronically by the client and an authorized Oficio Taller representative. Electronic signatures carry the same legal weight as physical signatures under Mexican federal law (NOM-151-SCFI).
